title: The Feedback Loop
author: From: High Output Management
content: The value of any process is determined by its feedback loop. If your system takes too long to show you the result of your actions, you are flying blind. Shorten the cycle, even if it hurts.

title: The Second-Order Effect
author: From: Thinking in Systems
content: Every intervention in a system has a ripple effect. If you force a change, look for where the 'pressure' will manifest next. Good system managers anticipate the backlash before it happens.
action: Forecast the backlash to your next plan.

title: The Cognitive Miser
author: From: Predictably Irrational
content: Your brain will choose the path of least resistance every time. If you want to think clearly, you must force System 2 thinking (deliberate, effortful analysis) by creating artificial friction.

title: The Sunk Cost Fallacy
author: From: The Art of Thinking Clearly
content: "I've come too far to turn back" is the mantra of losers. Future value is the only metric that matters. If the current trajectory isn't meeting the ROI, drop it—yesterday.

title: The Principle of Asymmetry
author: From: Antifragile
content: Look for opportunities with limited downside and high upside. If the cost of failure is fixed and the potential gain is exponential, the decision makes itself. Avoid the reverse.
